Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and N is [EN] cM (EN)</li><li>The distance between genes E and P is [EP] cM (EP)</li><li>The distance between genes N and P is [NP] cM (NP)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EN	32		EP	10		NP	24		geneorder	epn	npe
FIB_PLUS	<p>6b3d_eaf1</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene C</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene N</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene X</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and N is [CN] cM (CN)</li><li>The distance between genes C and X is [CX] cM (CX)</li><li>The distance between genes N and X is [NX] cM (NX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CN	5		CX	16		NX	20		geneorder	ncx	xcn

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #845100;">Gene A</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #845100;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #845100;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #007042;">Gene B</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #007042;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #007042;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene N</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">a</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #006883;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and B is [AB] cM (AB)</li><li>The distance between genes A and N is [AN] cM (AN)</li><li>The distance between genes B and N is [BN] cM (BN)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AB	4		AN	15		BN	18		geneorder	ban	nab
FIB_PLUS	<p>c1e6_4ac8</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene C</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006883;">Gene D</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006883;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006883;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #903383;">Gene F</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #903383;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #903383;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and D is [CD] cM (CD)</li><li>The distance between genes C and F is [CF] cM (CF)</li><li>The distance between genes D and F is [DF] cM (DF)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CD	25		CF	6		DF	20		geneorder	cfd	dfc

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene C</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #8d4820;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene E</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene J</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #2a58ba;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and E is [CE] cM (CE)</li><li>The distance between genes C and J is [CJ] cM (CJ)</li><li>The distance between genes E and J is [EJ] cM (EJ)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CE	5		CJ	29		EJ	25		geneorder	cej	jec
FIB_PLUS	<p>554b_e68a</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene B</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #06639a;">Gene J</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #06639a;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #06639a;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #98345b;">Gene X</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #98345b;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #98345b;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and J is [BJ] cM (BJ)</li><li>The distance between genes B and X is [BX] cM (BX)</li><li>The distance between genes J and X is [JX] cM (JX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BJ	15		BX	6		JX	10		geneorder	bxj	jxb
FIB_PLUS	<p>212d_2ef9</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene J</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #8d4820;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene M</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #7b3ba4;">Gene W</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #7b3ba4;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7b3ba4;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes J and M is [JM] cM (JM)</li><li>The distance between genes J and W is [JW] cM (JW)</li><li>The distance between genes M and W is [MW] cM (MW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	JM	9		JW	31		MW	25		geneorder	jmw	wmj
FIB_PLUS	<p>ef81_63d9</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene D</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene N</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene T</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and N is [DN] cM (DN)</li><li>The distance between genes D and T is [DT] cM (DT)</li><li>The distance between genes N and T is [NT] cM (NT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DN	16		DT	5		NT	12		geneorder	dtn	ntd
FIB_PLUS	<p>d3d4_5803</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene D</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #06639a;">Gene H</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #06639a;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #06639a;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #98345b;">Gene W</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #98345b;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #98345b;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">d</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #006883;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and H is [DH] cM (DH)</li><li>The distance between genes D and W is [DW] cM (DW)</li><li>The distance between genes H and W is [HW] cM (HW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DH	16		DW	34		HW	25		geneorder	dhw	whd

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene E</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene H</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006883;">Gene Y</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #006883;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006883;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and H is [EH] cM (EH)</li><li>The distance between genes E and Y is [EY] cM (EY)</li><li>The distance between genes H and Y is [HY] cM (HY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EH	22		EY	39		HY	25		geneorder	ehy	yhe

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene A</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #396d15;">Gene F</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #396d15;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #396d15;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006883;">Gene N</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #006883;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and F is [AF] cM (AF)</li><li>The distance between genes A and N is [AN] cM (AN)</li><li>The distance between genes F and N is [FN] cM (FN)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AF	25		AN	12		FN	15		geneorder	anf	fna
FIB_PLUS	<p>45a6_395a</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene D</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene E</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #06639a;">Gene P</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #06639a;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #06639a;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and E is [DE] cM (DE)</li><li>The distance between genes D and P is [DP] cM (DP)</li><li>The distance between genes E and P is [EP] cM (EP)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DE	21		DP	37		EP	25		geneorder	dep	ped
FIB_PLUS	<p>5345_03e1</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene M</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #8d4820;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #007042;">Gene P</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #007042;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #007042;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene R</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #2a58ba;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes M and P is [MP] cM (MP)</li><li>The distance between genes M and R is [MR] cM (MR)</li><li>The distance between genes P and R is [PR] cM (PR)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	MP	9		MR	25		PR	31		geneorder	pmr	rmp
FIB_PLUS	<p>a641_b6a1</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene D</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #8d4820;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #396d15;">Gene E</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #396d15;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #396d15;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #7b3ba4;">Gene F</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #7b3ba4;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7b3ba4;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and E is [DE] cM (DE)</li><li>The distance between genes D and F is [DF] cM (DF)</li><li>The distance between genes E and F is [EF] cM (EF)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DE	34		DF	11		EF	25		geneorder	dfe	efd

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene B</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene C</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006b71;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #98345b;">Gene P</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #98345b;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #98345b;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">b</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #006883;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and C is [BC] cM (BC)</li><li>The distance between genes B and P is [BP] cM (BP)</li><li>The distance between genes C and P is [CP] cM (CP)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BC	32		BP	18		CP	20		geneorder	bpc	cpb
FIB_PLUS	<p>5bf6_15aa</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene J</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006883;">Gene P</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #006883;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006883;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #903383;">Gene X</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #903383;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #903383;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes J and P is [JP] cM (JP)</li><li>The distance between genes J and X is [JX] cM (JX)</li><li>The distance between genes P and X is [PX] cM (PX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	JP	7		JX	16		PX	10		geneorder	jpx	xpj

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene D</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #396d15;">Gene E</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #396d15;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #396d15;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene Y</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #2a58ba;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and E is [DE] cM (DE)</li><li>The distance between genes D and Y is [DY] cM (DY)</li><li>The distance between genes E and Y is [EY] cM (EY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DE	4		DY	15		EY	18		geneorder	edy	yde
FIB_PLUS	<p>b53c_7497</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene E</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene R</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006b71;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #903383;">Gene W</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #903383;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #903383;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and R is [ER] cM (ER)</li><li>The distance between genes E and W is [EW] cM (EW)</li><li>The distance between genes R and W is [RW] cM (RW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	ER	10		EW	21		RW	28		geneorder	rew	wer
FIB_PLUS	<p>a2ec_ed9f</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #845100;">Gene J</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #845100;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #845100;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene R</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #7b3ba4;">Gene X</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #7b3ba4;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7b3ba4;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes J and R is [JR] cM (JR)</li><li>The distance between genes J and X is [JX] cM (JX)</li><li>The distance between genes R and X is [RX] cM (RX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	JR	5		JX	16		RX	20		geneorder	rjx	xjr
FIB_PLUS	<p>aef9_3301</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #845100;">Gene A</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #845100;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #845100;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene D</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene W</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and D is [AD] cM (AD)</li><li>The distance between genes A and W is [AW] cM (AW)</li><li>The distance between genes D and W is [DW] cM (DW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AD	37		AW	20		DW	25		geneorder	awd	dwa

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene M</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene R</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006b71;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene T</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">m</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #006883;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes M and R is [MR] cM (MR)</li><li>The distance between genes M and T is [MT] cM (MT)</li><li>The distance between genes R and T is [RT] cM (RT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	MR	8		MT	25		RT	32		geneorder	rmt	tmr

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene C</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene E</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene F</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and E is [CE] cM (CE)</li><li>The distance between genes C and F is [CF] cM (CF)</li><li>The distance between genes E and F is [EF] cM (EF)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CE	16		CF	25		EF	35		geneorder	ecf	fce

FIB_PLUS	<p>f4c6_af4e</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene C</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene K</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006b71;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #903383;">Gene P</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #903383;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #903383;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and K is [CK] cM (CK)</li><li>The distance between genes C and P is [CP] cM (CP)</li><li>The distance between genes K and P is [KP] cM (KP)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CK	39		CP	16		KP	25		geneorder	cpk	kpc
FIB_PLUS	<p>adb2_3e9c</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene E</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene M</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene W</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006b71;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and M is [EM] cM (EM)</li><li>The distance between genes E and W is [EW] cM (EW)</li><li>The distance between genes M and W is [MW] cM (MW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EM	9		EW	25		MW	30		geneorder	mew	wem
FIB_PLUS	<p>0cb3_1254</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene E</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #007042;">Gene T</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #007042;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #007042;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene X</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and T is [ET] cM (ET)</li><li>The distance between genes E and X is [EX] cM (EX)</li><li>The distance between genes T and X is [TX] cM (TX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	ET	33		EX	15		TX	24		geneorder	ext	txe

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene N</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene P</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene W</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse;
