Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and R is [AR] cM (AR)</li><li>The distance between genes A and Y is [AY] cM (AY)</li><li>The distance between genes R and Y is [RY] cM (RY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AR	16		AY	25		RY	34		geneorder	ray	yar
FIB_PLUS	<p>9cfa_76cf</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #396d15;">Gene H</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #396d15;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #396d15;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene P</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #2a58ba;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #98345b;">Gene Y</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #98345b;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #98345b;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes H and P is [HP] cM (HP)</li><li>The distance between genes H and Y is [HY] cM (HY)</li><li>The distance between genes P and Y is [PY] cM (PY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	HP	28		HY	10		PY	22		geneorder	hyp	pyh
FIB_PLUS	<p>5586_355a</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene N</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #8d4820;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene P</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene X</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #2a58ba;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes N and P is [NP] cM (NP)</li><li>The distance between genes N and X is [NX] cM (NX)</li><li>The distance between genes P and X is [PX] cM (PX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	NP	20		NX	39		PX	25		geneorder	npx	xpn
FIB_PLUS	<p>7392_2b51</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene E</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene H</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene Y</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #2a58ba;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #2a58ba;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes E and H is [EH] cM (EH)</li><li>The distance between genes E and Y is [EY] cM (EY)</li><li>The distance between genes H and Y is [HY] cM (HY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	EH	23		EY	4		HY	20		geneorder	eyh	hye

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene K</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene T</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006883;">Gene Y</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #006883;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006883;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes K and T is [KT] cM (KT)</li><li>The distance between genes K and Y is [KY] cM (KY)</li><li>The distance between genes T and Y is [TY] cM (TY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	KT	18		KY	20		TY	34		geneorder	tky	ykt

FIB_PLUS	<p>5de3_ff6b</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #007042;">Gene A</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #007042;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #007042;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #06639a;">Gene E</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #06639a;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #06639a;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #903383;">Gene Y</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #903383;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #903383;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and E is [AE] cM (AE)</li><li>The distance between genes A and Y is [AY] cM (AY)</li><li>The distance between genes E and Y is [EY] cM (EY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AE	30		AY	9		EY	25		geneorder	aye	eya
FIB_PLUS	<p>f0ae_b807</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene D</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #8d4820;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #396d15;">Gene F</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #396d15;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #396d15;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006883;">Gene R</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #006883;"><i>rusty</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006883;">Gene R</span> colonies develop a reddish-brown pigmentation, reminiscent of rusted metal.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and F is [DF] cM (DF)</li><li>The distance between genes D and R is [DR] cM (DR)</li><li>The distance between genes F and R is [FR] cM (FR)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DF	33		DR	12		FR	25		geneorder	drf	frd
FIB_PLUS	<p>e608_7ed4</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene F</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene P</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006883;">Gene W</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #006883;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006883;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes F and P is [FP] cM (FP)</li><li>The distance between genes F and W is [FW] cM (FW)</li><li>The distance between genes P and W is [PW] cM (PW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	FP	18		FW	5		PW	14		geneorder	fwp	pwf
FIB_PLUS	<p>4535_7b8a</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene A</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene K</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #06639a;">Gene M</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #06639a;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #06639a;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and K is [AK] cM (AK)</li><li>The distance between genes A and M is [AM] cM (AM)</li><li>The distance between genes K and M is [KM] cM (KM)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AK	28		AM	10		KM	22		geneorder	amk	kma
FIB_PLUS	<p>979d_82d3</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene C</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006883;">Gene W</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #006883;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006883;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #903383;">Gene X</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #903383;"><i>xenon</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #903383;">Gene X</span> cells emit a faint glow under UV light, as if they were fluorescent.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: monospace;">c</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; font-family: monospace; color: #006883;">+</span></td> <td align="center" style="border: 0px;"><span style="font-size: large; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and W is [CW] cM (CW)</li><li>The distance between genes C and X is [CX] cM (CX)</li><li>The distance between genes W and X is [WX] cM (WX)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CW	33		CX	12		WX	25		geneorder	cxw	wxc

The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene A</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene D</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #903383;">Gene K</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #903383;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #903383;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and D is [AD] cM (AD)</li><li>The distance between genes A and K is [AK] cM (AK)</li><li>The distance between genes D and K is [DK] cM (DK)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AD	22		AK	8		DK	15		geneorder	akd	dka

FIB_PLUS	<p>4047_6bd0</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene D</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene H</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006b71;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #98345b;">Gene W</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #98345b;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #98345b;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and H is [DH] cM (DH)</li><li>The distance between genes D and W is [DW] cM (DW)</li><li>The distance between genes H and W is [HW] cM (HW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DH	14		DW	38		HW	25		geneorder	dhw	whd
FIB_PLUS	<p>358a_415f</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene F</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #8d4820;"><i>fuzzy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene F</span> colonies are covered in soft, fine filaments, giving them a fuzzy, cotton-like texture.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene J</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #006d5e;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006d5e;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene K</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes F and J is [FJ] cM (FJ)</li><li>The distance between genes F and K is [FK] cM (FK)</li><li>The distance between genes J and K is [JK] cM (JK)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	FJ	14		FK	38		JK	25		geneorder	fjk	kjf
FIB_PLUS	<p>b341_c69a</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene C</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>clumpy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene C</span> grows in dense, irregular clusters, with cells clumping together rather than spreading smoothly.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene D</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006b71;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene J</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes C and D is [CD] cM (CD)</li><li>The distance between genes C and J is [CJ] cM (CJ)</li><li>The distance between genes D and J is [DJ] cM (DJ)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	CD	8		CJ	30		DJ	25		geneorder	cdj	jdc
FIB_PLUS	<p>9dc0_ae66</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #845100;">Gene B</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #845100;"><i>bubbly</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #845100;">Gene B</span> produces excessive gas bubbles during growth, causing foamy appearance of the media.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #007042;">Gene E</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #007042;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #007042;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #06639a;">Gene W</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #06639a;"><i>webbed</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #06639a;">Gene W</span> colonies produce delicate, web-like strands that connect neighboring colonies in a cobweb pattern.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes B and E is [BE] cM (BE)</li><li>The distance between genes B and W is [BW] cM (BW)</li><li>The distance between genes E and W is [EW] cM (EW)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	BE	9		BW	20		EW	27		geneorder	ebw	wbe
FIB_PLUS	<p>4f81_1d9d</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene A</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>amber</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene A</span> cells develop a rich yellow-orange pigmentation, giving the colony a warm, amber hue.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006883;">Gene D</span></strong> is connected with the '<span style="color: #006883;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006883;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #7b3ba4;">Gene P</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #7b3ba4;"><i>pebble</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #7b3ba4;">Gene P</span> produces colonies with a rough, uneven surface that resembles a collection of tiny pebbles.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes A and D is [AD] cM (AD)</li><li>The distance between genes A and P is [AP] cM (AP)</li><li>The distance between genes D and P is [DP] cM (DP)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	AD	9		AP	25		DP	33		geneorder	dap	pad
FIB_PLUS	<p>e3a6_7aa4</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #735b00;">Gene H</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #735b00;"><i>hairy</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #735b00;">Gene H</span> cells develop long, thread-like filaments that extend outward, creating a hairy, shaggy texture on the colony.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene M</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006b71;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #98345b;">Gene Y</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #98345b;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #98345b;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes H and M is [HM] cM (HM)</li><li>The distance between genes H and Y is [HY] cM (HY)</li><li>The distance between genes M and Y is [MY] cM (MY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	HM	27		HY	10		MY	20		geneorder	hym	myh

FIB_PLUS	<p>a553_c492</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene J</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #9c3732;"><i>jeweled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #9c3732;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene K</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>knotted</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene T</span></strong> is associated with the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes J and K is [JK] cM (JK)</li><li>The distance between genes J and T is [JT] cM (JT)</li><li>The distance between genes K and T is [KT] cM (KT)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	JK	35		JT	16		KT	25		geneorder	jtk	ktj
FIB_PLUS	<p>5139_107d</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene N</span></strong> is affiliated with the '<span style="color: #8d4820;"><i>nude</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #8d4820;">Gene N</span> cells have an unusually smooth surface with no visible external features or textures.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #007042;">Gene T</span></strong> is linked with the '<span style="color: #007042;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #007042;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #06639a;">Gene Y</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #06639a;"><i>yolk</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #06639a;">Gene Y</span> cells develop a dense, yellowish core that resembles an egg yolk when viewed under a microscope.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes N and T is [NT] cM (NT)</li><li>The distance between genes N and Y is [NY] cM (NY)</li><li>The distance between genes T and Y is [TY] cM (TY)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	NT	26		NY	8		TY	20		geneorder	nyt	tyn
FIB_PLUS	<p>b678_28ae</p> <h5>Unordered Tetrad Three Gene Mapping</h5><p>In this problem, you will use unordered tetrads to determine the order of three genes and calculate the distances between them. The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> is used in this study. A cross has been performed to study the linkage relationships among three genes, and the resulting genotypes are summarized in the table below.</p><h6>Characteristics of Recessive Phenotypes</h6><p><ul><li><strong><span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene D</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #5f6310;"><i>doubled</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5f6310;">Gene D</span> cells display double or multiple budding, with several buds emerging simultaneously.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #006b71;">Gene E</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #006b71;"><i>elephant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #006b71;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene T</span></strong> is correlated with the '<span style="color: #5c4cb0;"><i>toxic</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #5c4cb0;">Gene T</span> secretes a toxic compound that inhibits or kills other microbial colonies nearby.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse; Once calculated, fill in the following four blanks: </p><ul><li>The distance between genes D and E is [DE] cM (DE)</li><li>The distance between genes D and T is [DT] cM (DT)</li><li>The distance between genes E and T is [ET] cM (ET)</li><li>From this, the correct order of the genes is [geneorder] (gene order).</li></ul><h6>Step-by-Step Instructions</h6><ul> <li>Step 1: Find the row for the Parental Type for all three genes.</li> <li>Step 2: Pick any two genes and assign PD, NPD, TT.</li> <li>Step 3: Determine if the two genes are linked.</li> <ul><li>PD &gt;&gt; NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul> <li>Step 4: Determine the map distance between the two genes.</li> <ul><li>D = &half; (TT + 6 NPD) / total = (3 NPD + &half; TT) / total</li></ul> <li>Step 5: Go back to Step 2 and pick a new pair of genes until all pairs are complete.</li></ul><h6>Important Answer Guidelines</h6><p><ul><li><i>Important Tip 1:</i> Your calculated distances between each pair of genes should be a whole number. Finding a decimal in your answer, such as 5.5, indicates a mistake was made. Please provide your answer as a complete number without fractions or decimals.</li><li><i>Important Tip 2:</i> Your answer should be written as a numerical value only, with no spaces, commas, or units such as "cM" or "map units". For example, if the distance is fifty one centimorgans, simply write "51".</li><li><i>Important Tip 3:</i> Your gene order answer should be written as three letters only, with no spaces, commas, hyphens, or other characters allowed. For example, if the gene order is B - A - C, simply write "bac" or "cab".</li></ul></p>	DE	35		DT	12		ET	25		geneorder	dte	etd
